name: Peanut Butter Play Dough
directions: Combine all in a bowl.
Mix with hands until smooth.
Make sure to wash hands before playing with this and be on a clean counter if you expect that the kids will be eating this while playing.

name: Play Dough
directions: Mix all ingredients in saucepan.
Cook on medium heat until thick.
Store in airtight container.

similar.name: Play Doh - Play Dough  (No Stove Top Cooking Required)

name: Grandma Terri's  Play Dough
directions: Mix all ingredients together in a sauce pan.
Cook on medium heat stirring CONSTANTLY until it gets in a ball and is no longer sticky.
Seal in an airtight container and let the creativity flow.
In an airtight container it will last for months.

similar.name: Play Doh - Play Dough  (No Stove Top Cooking Required)

name: Make & Eat Play Dough
directions: In blender, grind flour and oatmeal 30 seconds; place in lg bowl; add water and knead briefly.
Add corn syrup, peanut butter, dry milk, and sugar; mix well, kneading w/ hands.
Add additional flour or dry milk if dough is too sticky to knead.
Note:  Children can make things w/ the “dough” using chips, M&Ms, peanuts, & raisins to decorate.
Note 2:  If you're brave, let children help you make it!

name: The Best  Play Dough Recipe
directions: Mix flour and salt in a large bowl.
In a glass measuring cup, mix alum, water, oil, and color and add to the flour/salt mixture.  Stir until slightly cool.
Knead well, adding flour until it stops sticking to your hands.
Keep in an airtight container when not in use.

similar.name: Play Doh - Play Dough  (No Stove Top Cooking Required)

name: Kool-Aid Play Dough
directions: 1. Mix first four ingredients to pan, then mix in last two. Cook over medium heat 3-5 minutes until it forms a ball. Remove; let cool a bit; knead.

similar.name: Play Doh - Play Dough  (No Stove Top Cooking Required)

name: Halloween Play Dough
directions: First you need to boil your water. When it's got a nice rolling boil going on, add your flour, salt, oil and cream of tarter immediately. Stir with a wooden spoon. Turn off the burner and move the pot to the counter.

After it cools, you want to roll it by hand to get it all smooth. Now you want to add some color. You can to dye it spooky colors with food coloring. Just mix the colors into the dough until you get the desired color.

To make the color orange, you need to mix red and yellow food coloring. If you want purple, mix red and blue. For black, mix all the colors in your food coloring together and this will give you black or gray. For the monster color, dark blackish green, mix all the colors together but add more green then any other color.

Set up a clean area for your child to play with their play dough. Since this is HALLOWEEN play dough, suggest to your child that they make Halloween creations, such as pumpkins, a witch or even a creepy monster. You can buy the cheaper plastic Halloween cookie cutters that work perfect as play dough toys.
Storing your play dough immediately after use is a very important thing. If you don't, it will dry out right away. You can store your playdough in an airtight container or in Ziploc bags.

I recommend storing your different colored play dough separately, otherwise you will end up with a big brown gob. Homemade play dough will only last a few days but it's very easy to cook up a new batch.

similar.name: Play Doh - Play Dough  (No Stove Top Cooking Required)

name: Play Doh - Play Dough  (No Stove Top Cooking Required)
directions: Put in all dry ingredients (flour, cream of tartar and salt) into a large heat resistant bowl - Mix ingredients.
In another large heat resistant bowl add all the wet ingredients (oil, your child’s favourite colour using the food colouring & hot boiled water) stir ingredients.
Add wet ingredients with dry ingredients and mix well.
Take Care as it is HOT.  Tip mixture out on a floured bench and knead. Add extra flour until dough is smooth and not sticking on bench, kneading could take about 15 minutes.
Stored in air tight containers in the fridge.

name: A-B-C Grahams
directions: Heat oven to 350. In a large food processor fitted with a metal blade or by hand, mix flour, brown sugar, wheat germ, baking soda, salt, and cinnamon. Cover and process until well combined. Add butter (use the tines of a fork if mixing by hand). Cover and process until fine crumbs form. Add honey, milk, and vanilla. Cover and process until mixture starts to hold together. If necessary, place in a bowl and work with your hands until dough is smooth. Divide in half and flatten each half to a disk.

On a lightly floured work surface, roll each half of dough to a 9"x8"-inch rectangle. Cut each into 18 3"x1 1/3"-inch rectangles. Arrange them 1"-inch apart on ungreased cookie sheets. Using the flat end of a skewer, poke an A-B-C design in each rectangle. Sprinkle each lightly with granulated sugar.
Bake about 8 minutes or until lightly browned. Transfer cookies to wire racks, immediately re-poke A-B-C design if necessary, and let cool completely.

name: Frosting Dough
directions: Mix and knead.

name: Cookie Dough (From Greek Yogurt)
directions: Open container of yogurt and mix in all ingredients. ENJOY! It is that easy. :).
